The '''S'krrr''' were native to a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[S'krrr|same name]]. They were tall mantis-like insectoids with sharp body segments and small transparent, vestigial wings on their backs. S'krrr could not fly, because their small wings were unable to carry their weight. They would use their wings for a form of communication known as "wing-song".

[[File:Rabaanite_SWJ9.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A S'krrr fights a Rabaanite.]]
There had once been a rivalry between the S'krrr and the [[Human/Legends|Humans]] of the neighboring planet, [[Rabaan]] (known as [[Rabaanite]]s). One-on-one combat would often take place on the [[Combat Moon]]. Even after this ended, some S'krrr continued to practice melee combat with [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]]. Despite remaining technologically advanced for a people on such a remote planet, they never developed interstellar travel. Some S'krrr worshiped the lower insects of the planet, such as [[drog]] beetles, because they believed they were descended from insects such as these.

{{Species-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The S'krrr were created by [[John Whitman]] in 1996 for his short story ''Combat Moon''. Their only other appearance came in 1998 when Whitman featured them in book 8 of the ''Galaxy of Fear'' series he was writing.
